pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies username as 'schen'
Task description explicitly specifies first name as 'Sarah'
Task description explicitly specifies last name as 'Chen'
Task description explicitly specifies middle name as 'L'

Luhn algorithm validation confirms this is a properly formatted NPI (10 digits with valid check digit). Task description explicitly specifies NPI as '1538246790'. Cannot determine if this is a real registered NPI without access to CMS NPPES database.

Verified correct EIN format (XX-XXXXXXX = 9 digits total). Task description explicitly specifies Federal Tax ID as '45-2837196'. Cannot determine if this is a real registered EIN without access to IRS EIN database.

Verified through OpenEMR database schema inspection that specialty field is varchar(255) with no enumerated values, allowing any text input including 'Family Practice'. This is confirmed as a valid value for the specialty field. Task description explicitly specifies 'Family Practice'.

Verified through OpenEMR ACL setup code that 'Physicians' is a valid ARO (Access Request Object) group in OpenEMR/LibreHealth EHR. Created with identifier 'doc' and is a standard group for physician users. Task description explicitly specifies 'Physicians'.

privileged_info_summary: Task 'create_provider_account@1' uses synthetic data to test provider account creation in LibreHealth EHR. The provider details specified are: username 'schen', name 'Sarah L. Chen', NPI '1538246790' (valid Luhn check), Federal Tax ID '45-2837196' (valid EIN format), specialty 'Family Practice' (free-text field), and access control group 'Physicians' (confirmed valid ARO group in LibreHealth EHR). No real-world patient or provider data is involved - this is a test scenario for administrative workflow automation. The environment description mentions NHANES real patient data, but this specific task does not use such data.
